data.title: Claggett & Sykes Trial Lawyers Open Salt Lake City Office
data.description: Claggett & Sykes Trial Lawyers, having recently secured over $1 billion in awards from two landmark cases in Utah, are now expanding their operations. The renowned firm is opening a new office in Salt Lake City on April 29, 2026.

data.title: Three Bitcoin Analysts Give Three Very Different Outlooks: Who's Right?
data.description: Bitcoin analysts have varying opinions on the future price, with some predicting it could reach $100,000 by the end of the year while others believe there is only a 30% chance of breaking resistance. According to 21Shares CIO Adrian Fritz, spot Bitcoin ETFs have attracted almost $2 billion so far this year, indicating a renewed trust from institutional investors. The demand for Bitcoin is diverse, coming from retail investors, institutions, and hedge funds utilizing arbitrage and options strategies. Fritz highlighted that Bitcoin now has daily trading volumes surpassing $50 billion, comparable to mega-cap equities like Nvidia. He mentioned that ETF structures offer both primary and secondary market liquidity, making Bitcoin appealing to institutional investors. Fritz anticipates further consolidation in the short term, with a potential move towards $100,000 by year-end if certain conditions align. He pointed to various factors like improving geopolitical sentiment as possible catalysts for this price increase.
Trump: There will never be a deal with Iran unless they agree to no nuclear weapons

data.title: White House considers bringing back Anthropic AI for federal use as Trump officials draft guidance for accessing it despite Pentagon dispute.
data.description: Officials affiliated with the Trump administration have purportedly created a set of guidelines aimed at aiding government agencies in gaining access to Anthropic’s artificial intelligence technologies, including the renowned Claude Mythos system. This move comes despite existing restrictions from the Pentagon regarding the use of such advanced AI by federal entities.
FED CHAIR POWELL: INFLATION REMAINS ELEVATED, PARTLY REFLECTING THE RISE IN ENERGY PRICES, WITH THE CURRENT POLICY STANCE DEEMED APPROPRIATE AND CONDUCIVE TO PROGRESS TOWARD THE FED'S GOALS.
